title: Privacy policy
metaDescription: GDPR privacy policy: loyalty & reviews, purposes, sub-processors, retention, multi-establishment, STOP, import, AI.
intro: Last updated: April 2026. Version: 1.3 — single “Loyalty & Reviews” policy. This document describes the processing of personal data related to review solicitation (WhatsApp, technical queue) and the loyalty programme (points, rewards, birthdays, till and terminal). It is consistent with the record of processing activities (Article 30 GDPR) published on this site. AI features may produce incorrect outputs; they are indicative and must be validated by the User before distribution (see Section 8). No system guarantees absolute security or compliance; REPUTEXA applies proportionate measures and updates this policy when processing or regulation changes.

toc.label: 7. Zenith plan — Review collection and end-customer data
8. AI assurance — Data not used for training

responsable_content:
The controller of personal data within the meaning of Articles 4 and 26 GDPR is REPUTEXA, publisher of reputexa.fr, whose registered office is in France. Processing is documented in the record of processing activities (Article 30 GDPR), available on this site. REPUTEXA applies the principles of data minimisation, storage limitation and purpose limitation.

For any question about processing: contact@reputexa.fr.
roles_rgpd_content: REPUTEXA is controller for data relating to the management of its own customers (user accounts, billing, support). REPUTEXA acts as processor (Article 28 GDPR) on behalf of its Users (Merchants) for: sending WhatsApp solicitations, the review queue, loyalty and reward programmes (points, tiers, birthday gifts, related campaigns), importing customer lists and till- or terminal-connected features, under documented instructions from the merchant (account settings, loyalty rules, imported files). The Merchant remains solely responsible for loyalty strategy, scales, validated messages and legal bases vis-à-vis its own customers. End-customer data are processed only to execute those instructions. Processing may be pooled across establishments belonging to the same controller so that the customer can use benefits across the network (points usable across several shops of the same operator, depending on configuration). Records of consent or refusal (STOP) may be kept for compliance evidence (technical logging).
donnees_content: We collect in particular: professional email address for the account and authentication; billing identifiers and payment data processed by Stripe; review content and data from your Google Business Profile when you connect the Google API; first name, last name, business phone, establishment name, address; language preference; technical data (IP address, server logs, session identifiers, cookies strictly necessary for operation and security). For the Zenith plan and loyalty: data transmitted by your systems (till, e-commerce, terminal) via webhooks or entry (first name, phone, purchase context or amount, number of items where applicable, delivery status where applicable), balance or points and reward history, date of birth or birthday month when you collect them for related benefits, postal address or city if you record them for the customer relationship. File import (CSV, Excel, etc.): columns you map (identity, phone, purchases, etc.) are processed to feed your loyalty database or campaigns, under your responsibility for the lawfulness of the initial collection (see also our Terms, import clause).
locale_content: To improve your experience and offer interface, communications and support in your language, we may infer a language preference from your phone country code (e.g. +33 for France, +39 for Italy, +34 for Spain, +49 for Germany). This is used only to personalise the language of the interface, emails and payment portal. You can change language at any time in your account settings.
finalites_content: Data are processed for: performance of the contract and provision of the service (account, subscription, subscribed features); management of the Merchant’s loyalty programme: calculation and tracking of points, allocation of rewards (tiers, birthdays, one-off offers), personalisation of the customer relationship, multi-site management where the same controller operates several establishments; legitimate interest (security, fraud prevention, service improvement, technical audience measurement where anonymised); consent where required (WhatsApp solicitations, non-essential cookies, commercial communications depending on your configuration); legal obligations (retention of invoices for accounting and tax purposes, responses to authorities). Specific purposes vis-à-vis end customers are defined and communicated by the Merchant (point of sale, GTC, shop policy).
sous_traitants_content: Your data may be processed by the following sub-processors, strictly within their mission and under documented instruction: Stripe (payments, subscriptions, billing); Resend (transactional email); OpenAI and/or Anthropic (response suggestions, audio transcription where applicable); Supabase (database, authentication — project in EU region); Vercel (application hosting); Google (OAuth, Google Business Profile API); Twilio and/or Meta (WhatsApp depending on configuration); Cloudflare (Turnstile if enabled). These providers are bound by standard contractual clauses (GDPR) or equivalent safeguards under their public DPAs. The list of sub-processors may evolve: updates to this policy or your information area.
zenith_captation_title: 7. Zenith plan — Review collection and end-customer data
zenith_captation_content: The Zenith plan includes automated review collection (“Capture AI”) and may be combined with loyalty-related messages (benefits, reminders) according to rules configured by the Merchant. WhatsApp contacts comply with purposes and prior information defined by the Merchant (physical retail or, for online shops, after delivery when the webhook sends the appropriate status). REPUTEXA acts as processor (Article 28 GDPR); the Merchant remains controller of its customers’ data. For the same number and the same merchant account, a new review solicitation campaign is not triggered until 120 days after the start of the previous one (unless a different rule is displayed or configured). Data kept in the technical queue for review solicitation are short-lived: they are anonymised or deleted no later than 120 days after entering the queue (subject to objection traces or legal obligations), in line with operational service settings. After the anti re-solicitation period, a new review invitation may be scheduled for that number if it is not on an objection list. Loyalty data (points history, active account) follow different retention — see “Retention periods”. STOP reply or refusal: messages stop according to technical mechanisms; a broad objection or full withdrawal may result in closure of the loyalty account and loss of unused benefits, under conditions set by the Merchant and detailed under “Your rights”. Logging of consents/refusals: technical traces for evidence.
ia_content:
Content sent for suggestion generation (review replies, WhatsApp or SMS drafts, establishment context) is processed by AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic or equivalents) solely to provide the feature. Subject to current contractual policies, REPUTEXA configures usage in line with “no training” / zero retention options where available. You should verify vendors’ DPAs for legal detail. Do not submit unnecessary or sensitive data.

Model outputs may be incorrect, incomplete or unsuitable; they do not constitute legal, tax, commercial or reputation advice from REPUTEXA.

The User (Merchant) acknowledges that AI-generated content is suggestive and error-prone. The controller remains responsible for final validation before sending or publishing. REPUTEXA shall not be liable for consequences of distribution without sufficient human review (commercial commitments, discounts, promises, language or factual errors), subject to mandatory legal obligations. Further contractual limits and exclusions: see Terms of Use (“Liability”).
securite_content:
Data are processed with encryption in transit (TLS) and at-rest security measures consistent with sector practice (managed hosting such as Supabase). The project is configured in the European Union region where possible. For the Zenith plan, access to Merchants’ end-customer data is limited by design (automated processing, environment separation).

REPUTEXA implements a security policy proportionate to risk: access management, dependency updates, application log monitoring where the plan allows, and incident response under internal procedures. No system is infallible; if a security incident is likely to affect personal data, the measures under “Personal data breaches” apply.

Record of processing activities (Article 30 GDPR): https://reputexa.fr/docs/registre-rgpd-reputexa.html (adjust domain if needed).
cookies_content: We use cookies and local storage strictly necessary for the service (session, security preferences, possibly Cloudflare Turnstile captcha). Any non-essential cookie or tracker will be subject to your prior consent where regulation requires (consent banner). You can configure your browser to refuse cookies; some features may then be degraded.
transferts_content: Where sub-processors are located outside the European Union or processing takes place there (e.g. US AI providers), transfers are governed by GDPR instruments: European Commission standard contractual clauses, supplementary measures where applicable, and vendor policies. You may request a copy of applicable safeguards at contact@reputexa.fr.
durees_content:
We distinguish periods by purpose:

Review solicitation data (Zenith technical queue, excluding active loyalty account): short retention — identifying elements needed for the campaign are anonymised or deleted no later than 120 days after entering the queue, except aggregated or statistical data without direct identification; a new review invitation for the same number (not objecting) is only possible after the anti re-solicitation period described in the Zenith section (120 days from the start of the previous campaign).

Loyalty data (points programme, rewards, history with the Merchant): retained for the entire active loyalty account with the Merchant on the platform. Account deemed inactive in the absence of a qualifying purchase or recorded loyalty interaction for thirty-six (36) consecutive months; thereafter data may be deleted or anonymised according to Merchant procedures and service settings, subject to prior information where required by law and objection lists.

Merchant user account and billing: duration of the contract plus accounting and tax retention obligations (often ten years for supporting documents, depending on applicable law).

Objection list (STOP): retention of the number or its fingerprint to avoid further solicitation, with no fixed contractual limit for the objection itself.

Consent logs: retention for compliance evidence, for a reasonable period in view of disputes. Periods may be adjusted to comply with a legal obligation or court decision.
violations_content: In the event of a personal data breach likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of individuals, REPUTEXA documents the incident, analyses causes and, where regulation requires, notifies the competent supervisory authority (including the CNIL in France) within GDPR time limits, and informs data subjects where the breach is likely to result in a high risk. Internal procedures aim to limit network and application impact. Operational details (technical logs, internal IP addresses) may be disclosed to authorities under a legal obligation or a substantiated request, but are not published on this site.
portabilite_content: From your account Settings you can download a structured export of your data (JSON) and request definitive deletion of your account in accordance with the GDPR. The export includes in particular profile, reviews, queue, blocklist, loyalty data managed via your merchant account and related consent logs. Account deletion results in deletion or anonymisation of associated data subject to legal retention obligations.
droits_content:
Under the GDPR you have rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, objection (including to marketing) and the right to give instructions on the fate of your data after your death (under French law). For merchant accounts: contact@reputexa.fr. For end customers of a shop using REPUTEXA: contact the Merchant first; you may also use the erasure form hosted on this site (link at the bottom of this page). You may lodge a complaint with the CNIL (cnil.fr) or the supervisory authority of your country of residence. Minors must not use the service without authorisation from their parent or guardian.

WhatsApp, STOP and loyalty: replying STOP stops automatic messages of that type on the relevant channel. If you require full removal from all the Merchant’s contact lists or deletion of your loyalty account, stopping solicitations may entail closing the loyalty programme account and loss of unused points or benefits, in accordance with the Merchant’s loyalty rules and applicable law; request this at the point of sale or via the coordinates shown on the information notice.

Automated decisions and profiling (Article 22 GDPR): AI-generated suggestions are proposals to be validated by the professional before sending or publication; they do not produce legal effects vis-à-vis the end consumer without human validation by the Merchant.
contact_content: For any question about the protection of your data: contact@reputexa.fr. Response time: we aim to respond within one month, extendable by two months in complex cases, in accordance with the GDPR.
client_finaux_erasure_notice: If you were contacted via WhatsApp through the REPUTEXA service on behalf of a shop and wish to request erasure of your data without using WhatsApp (STOP), you can use the dedicated form (same number as used with the merchant):
